title: actaea obtusiloba
intro: Actaea obtusiloba, also known as black cohosh, is a perennial plant native to eastern North America, belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its roots, which contain triterpene glycosides, including actein and cimicifugine. Actaea obtusiloba has been used to alleviate various health issues, including menopausal symptoms, anxiety, and menstrual cramps, due to its ability to act as a natural estrogen regulator and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, black cohosh is often combined with other herbs and taken in capsule or tea form, with recommended dosages typically ranging from 20 to 80 milligrams per day.
